description:
A compliance analyst wants to keep a very small set of plain-text reports synchronised in a local “remote-mount” directory that represents a mounted share on a different host.  
  Your goal is to:

  1. Make sure the following two directories exist (create them if they do not already):
     • /home/user/compliance_reports/  
     • /home/user/remote_mount/compliance_staging/

  2. Synchronise every file that is currently present in /home/user/compliance_reports/ into
     /home/user/remote_mount/compliance_staging/ using a single rsync command.  
     • The synchronisation must be one-way (source → destination).  
     • Preserve timestamps and permissions (-a flag is sufficient).  
     • Do NOT delete anything on the destination that is not also on the source.

  3. Immediately after the rsync finishes, append an audit-trail text file recording **exactly what was copied.**

     • The audit file path MUST be  
       /home/user/compliance_audit_logs/rsync_audit.log  
       (create the compliance_audit_logs directory first if it does not exist).  

     • For every file that rsync actually transferred (i.e. really copied because it was new or updated), write **one** log line in the following *single-line* pipe-delimited format (no spaces except the one shown inside the timestamp):

       YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS|SOURCE=<absolute path>|DESTINATION=<absolute path>|ACTION=SYNC|RESULT=OK

       Example (the timestamp shown is mandatory – do NOT invent your own time):

       2023-01-01 00:00:00|SOURCE=/home/user/compliance_reports/report_Q1.txt|DESTINATION=/home/user/remote_mount/compliance_staging/report_Q1.txt|ACTION=SYNC|RESULT=OK

     • After you have written the per-file rows, write one **summary** line using the exact template below (all on one line):

       SUMMARY|TOTAL_FILES=<n>|SUCCESS=<n>|FAIL=0|DURATION_SECS=N/A

       where “<n>” is the number of files rsync actually copied.

     • The automated tests will check that:
       a. The two required directories exist.  
       b. The destination directory now contains *every* file that exists in the source directory.  
       c. /home/user/compliance_audit_logs/rsync_audit.log exists and ends with exactly the set of lines described above, including the literal timestamp “2023-01-01 00:00:00” and “DURATION_SECS=N/A”.  
       d. The TOTAL_FILES and SUCCESS counters both equal the number of file lines written just above the summary.

  A typical session therefore involves only two or three shell commands: one to ensure the audit directory exists, one rsync command, and one or two echo/printf commands to append the required lines to the log.

  Do not move or rename any files. Do not require sudo. No network calls are needed; everything happens in the user’s home directory.
truth:
Initial filesystem state provided to the agent:

  1. /home/user/compliance_reports/   (directory, 755)
     ├── report_Q1.txt  (file, 644, text content: "Quarter 1 compliance results – draft\n")
     └── report_Q2.txt  (file, 644, text content: "Quarter 2 compliance results – draft\n")

  2. /home/user/remote_mount/         (directory, 755)
     └── compliance_staging/          (directory, 755)
        (starts completely empty)

  3. /home/user/compliance_audit_logs/ does NOT exist at start-up.

  Target final state that the grader will verify:

  A. Both report_Q1.txt and report_Q2.txt exist under
     /home/user/remote_mount/compliance_staging/
     and their byte-for-byte contents are identical to those in
     /home/user/compliance_reports/.

  B. /home/user/compliance_audit_logs/rsync_audit.log exists, is a regular text file, and its **last three lines exactly** are:

  2023-01-01 00:00:00|SOURCE=/home/user/compliance_reports/report_Q1.txt|DESTINATION=/home/user/remote_mount/compliance_staging/report_Q1.txt|ACTION=SYNC|RESULT=OK
  2023-01-01 00:00:00|SOURCE=/home/user/compliance_reports/report_Q2.txt|DESTINATION=/home/user/remote_mount/compliance_staging/report_Q2.txt|ACTION=SYNC|RESULT=OK
  SUMMARY|TOTAL_FILES=2|SUCCESS=2|FAIL=0|DURATION_SECS=N/A

  (Each line ends with a single Unix newline character, \n.)

  C. Permissions: The log directory compliance_audit_logs must have at least 755, and the log file rsync_audit.log must have at least 644.

  No other files, directories or log entries are checked.
